semanticCore: Weighing, scale, measure, value, or gravity
quranicRelevance: Weighing deeds (Wazn), scale/balance (Mizan - 'wa aqeemoo al-wazna bil-qist'), and balanced things (Mawzoon).

keyDerivations.meaning: To weigh, measure out, or value

figurativeOrRareUsages: وَأَنْبَتْنَا فِيهَا مِنْ كُلِّ شَيْءٍ مَوْزُونٍ (15:19): And We caused to grow therein of everything proportioned/balanced.
أَقِيمُوا الوَزْنَ بِالقِسْطِ: Maintain the weight/scale with justice.
summaryEn: The root represents weight and balance, undergirding the scale of justice (Mizan), balanced growth in nature (Mawzoon), and the weighing of deeds.
summaryAr: يدل هذا الجذر على الوزن والعدالة، مؤصلاً لمفهوم الميزان القسط، والنمو الطبيعي الموزون في الكون، ووزن الصحف يوم القيامة.
rawText: الوَزْنُ A certain star in the left fore leg of Centaurus. (Kzw.) See حَضَارِ. اِتَّزَنَهُ 8 He took it, or received it, by weight. (S, * Mgh, Msb, K. *) See an ex. voce سَنْجَةٌ. زِنِىٌّ , rel. n. of زِنَةٌ. (S, art. وعد, q. v., voce عِدَةٌ.) مِيزَانٌ A weighing-instrument; (TA:) a balance; a pair of scales. === The weight of a thing. (K, &c.) See مِثْقَالٌ. هٰذَا يُوَازِنُ هٰذَا 3 This is equiponderant to this. (S.) وَازِنٌ : see رَاجِحٌ: heavy: (Msb:) or of full weight: (KL:) pl. وُزَّنٌ: see زَالٌّ. You say, دِرْهَمٌ وَازِنٌ (S) A full, or complete, dirhem: (so in a copy of the S:) [a dirhem of full weight: a heavy dirhem. (PS.) وَزَنَ 1 It (a thing) was heavy: (Msb:) or outweighed, or preponderated; syn. وَجَحَ. (TA.) وَزَّانٌ A weigher. (TA, in art. قسط.)
